MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jared Walder of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices, APC (310.929.4900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Linda L. Nusser of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415.986.5900 ext. 4387) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-20-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Jared Walder of the law offices of Strategic Legal Practices, APC (310.929.4900) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Linda L. Nusser of the law offices of Gordon & Rees LLP (415.986.5900 ext. 4387) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY's DEMURRER TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACITON OF PLAINTIFF'S UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT, for fraud in the complaint filed by plaintiff Mitzi Parr is SUSTAINED with 20 days leave to amend. If she can do so in good faith, Ms. Parr has leave to plead facts to avoid the 3-year statute of limitations of CCP 338. Paragraph 8 of the complaint alleges that Ms. Parr purchased the vehicle on October 31, 2012 and she filed this action more than 3 years later on November 1, 2016. Ms. Parr's allegations seeking to invoke tolling and delayed discovery doctrines are conclusory lacking any ultimate facts. Ms. Parr needs to allege facts to support her theories to avoid the statute of limitations. (Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 437; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808; McDonald v Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 102; Baker v. Beech Aircraft Corp. (1974) 39 Cal.App.3d 315, 321.) Ford's remaining arguments lack merit. Less particularity is required for pleading a fraud claim when the facts lie within Ford's knowledge. (Alfaro v. Comm. Housing Imp. Systems & Planning Ass'n (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) Paragraphs 39 and 42 of the complaint support a fraud theory of liability based on Ford's exclusive knowledge of the defects. "[W]here one party to a transaction has sole knowledge or access to material facts and knows that such facts are not known or reasonably discoverable by the other party, then a duty to disclose exists." (Shapiro v. Sutherland (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1534, 1544.) Paragraph 43 of the complaint also alleges a theory of recovery based on active concealment. Ford can conduct discovery to determine the scope of Ms. Parr's damages. The economic loss rule is inapplicable if Ms. Parr is able to plead a non-time barred fraud claim. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
